objectives: Understand sentences as meaningful expressions
Identify subjects and predicates in simple sentences
Recognize correct word order in sentences
Form interrogative sentences
Form imperative sentences
Form exclamatory sentences
Identify nouns, pronouns, and adjectives
Recognize verbs, adverbs, and prepositions
Understand conjunctions and interjections
Categorize words into their respective classes
Understand verbs as words indicating action or state
Recognize single-word and multi-word verbs
Differentiate between main verbs and auxiliary verbs
Identify primary auxiliaries (be, have, do)
Identify modal auxiliaries
Apply rules for subject-verb agreement
Use correct verb forms based on the subject
Differentiate between time and tense
Understand the three forms of English verbs
Recognize verb aspects: perfect and progressive
Use Present Simple for habits, facts, and future plans
Use Present Progressive for ongoing or temporary actions
Identify non-progressive (stative) verbs
Employ Past Simple for completed past actions
Use Past Progressive for ongoing past actions
Apply Past Perfect for earlier past actions
Understand Present Perfect for past actions with present relevance
Utilize modals (will/shall) to express future
Use 'be going to' for future plans and indications
Employ present tenses for fixed future arrangements
Understand primary (be, have, do) and modal auxiliaries
Differentiate between auxiliaries and main verbs

topics.title_en: Modal Verbs for Future and Obligation
objectives: Use shall and will to express future and intention
Apply should and ought to for advice and obligation
Utilize would and used to for past habits and requests

topics.title_en: Modal Verbs for Ability and Possibility
objectives: Employ can and could to express ability and permission
Use may and might for possibility and permission
Understand need and dare in negative and interrogative contexts

topics.title_en: Modal Verbs for Necessity and Inference
objectives: Use must to express strong obligation or logical conclusion
Differentiate between mustn't and needn't
Practice applying various modals in sentences
Define a noun phrase (NP)
Identify the head noun in an NP
Recognize determiners and adjectives in an NP
Identify possessives, ordinals, and cardinals
Understand quantifiers and adjective phrases
Recognize classifiers and pre-determiners
Practice forming noun phrases with various elements
Analyze the structure of complex noun phrases

chapters.title_en: Pre and Post Modifiers in the Noun Phrase

objectives: Understand the concept of pre-modifiers
Understand the concept of post-modifiers
Identify modifiers that add extra meaning to nouns
Recognize articles, demonstratives, and numerals
Identify quantifiers, possessives, and distributives
Understand multipliers, wh-words, adjectives, and participles
Identify nouns functioning as pre-modifiers
Identify adjectives and adverbs as post-modifiers
Recognize prepositional phrases and non-finite clauses
Understand finite clauses as post-modifiers
Apply various pre-modifiers in noun phrases
Apply various post-modifiers in noun phrases
Practice building complex noun phrases
Distinguish between nouns that can be counted and those that cannot
Categorize nouns as countable or uncountable
Learn rules for forming plurals of countable nouns
Apply subject-verb agreement rules for countable nouns
Understand agreement for group nouns and nouns ending in 's'
Use appropriate quantifiers for uncountable nouns
Understand units of measurement for uncountable items
Recognize nouns with different meanings in plural forms
Understand articles (a, an, the) and their uses
Identify demonstratives (this, that, these, those)
Recognize numerals (cardinals and ordinals)
Use quantifiers (many, much, some, any) correctly
Apply possessives (my, his, Raja's) in sentences
Understand distributives (each, every, either, neither)
Learn the correct order of multiple determiners
Identify and correct errors in determiner sequence
Master the usage of definite and indefinite articles
Identify situations where no article is used
Understand article usage with institutions and transport
Apply articles with specific times and geographical names
Understand attributive (before noun) use of adjectives
Understand predicative (after link verb) use of adjectives
Identify adjectives used only in specific positions
Recognize post-positioned adjectives
Understand adjectives with different meanings based on position
Identify nouns functioning as classifiers (adjectives)
Differentiate between -ed and -ing adjectives
Learn the accepted order of multiple adjectives
Use commas and 'and' correctly with adjectives
Form and use comparative and superlative degrees
Understand irregular forms of comparison
Apply comparisons of equality and double comparatives

Identify types of adverbs: manner, time, place, frequency, degree
Differentiate adverbs from adjectives after link verbs
Learn how adverbs are formed (e.g., adding -ly)
Understand adverb placement in sentences (front, mid, end positions)
Recognize the order of multiple adverbs

objectives: Differentiate adverbs with similar forms but different meanings
Understand the use of 'good' and 'well'
Form comparative and superlative degrees of adverbs
Understand adverbs like 'yet', 'still', 'already', 'ago'
Recognize focus adverbs (e.g., 'only', 'even', 'just')

Use contracted forms in negative sentences
